evals.prompt: /chrome-devtools-memory-leak-debugging reproduce the leak on the dashboard
evals.expected_output: Baseline + repeated-interaction target snapshot + final; analysis without loading raw heapsnapshot.
assertions: Agent dispatches to memory leak mode.
Captures baseline, target, final snapshots around repeated loop.
Never loads/reads raw .heapsnapshot content into context.
Uses memlab or bounded analyzer when appropriate, with user-approved commands.

evals.prompt: Page is slow due to memory bloat after 50 navigations.
evals.expected_output: Activates memory specific debugging not LCP or generic devtools.
assertions: Classifies symptom as memory leak / heap growth.
Does not start performance trace for LCP.

evals.expected_output: Refusal to read raw heapsnapshot; requires memlab or summary commands.
assertions: Agent refuses direct read of raw .heapsnapshot.
Cites: Never read raw .heapsnapshot files into context.
Suggests memlab-compatible or analyzer command with approval.

evals.prompt: There are many detached DOM nodes, clean them up
evals.expected_output: Treats detached as suspect; asks for confirmation they are not intentional caches before recommending removal.
assertions: Agent treats detached DOM as not automatically wrong.
Asks user before advising cache changes.
Cites critical rule on detached nodes.

evals.prompt: Node process RSS keeps growing under load, diagnose leak
evals.expected_output: Distinguishes Node.js memory from browser; prefers runtime-native or memlab.
assertions: Agent isolates client vs server-side leak possibility.
For Node prefers non-browser heap capture.
Still refuses to load raw snapshots directly.
